title: Can search and return match data from all provided CSV files
evidence: src/main/java/com/braziliansoccer/mcp/data/DataRepository.java:33-37 loads all 5 datasets; src/test/java/com/braziliansoccer/mcp/DataLoaderTest.java verifies all datasets load successfully; tests run: 23954 total matches loaded

title: Can search and return player data
evidence: src/main/java/com/braziliansoccer/mcp/data/DataLoader.java loads FIFA player database; search_players tool in BrazilianSoccerMcpServer.java:112-127; PlayerToolsTest.java tests player search functionality; 18207 players loaded

title: Can calculate basic statistics (wins, losses, goals)
evidence: TeamTools.java implements getTeamStats() for win/loss/draw calculation; get_team_stats tool in MCP server; TeamToolsTest verifies statistics computation; home/away breakdown supported

title: Can compare teams head-to-head
evidence: MatchTools.java implements headToHead() method; head_to_head tool in MCP server:67-80; MatchToolsTest::testHeadToHeadFlamengoFluminense verifies functionality with win statistics

title: Handles team name variations correctly
evidence: TeamNameNormalizer.java provides comprehensive mapping for 20+ teams with 60+ name variations including state suffixes, full names, and abbreviations; TeamNameNormalizerTest verifies normalization logic

title: Returns properly formatted responses
evidence: MCP tools return JSON-formatted responses; src/main/java/com/braziliansoccer/mcp/tools/ implement formatted output methods; tests validate response structure

title: All CSV files are loadable and queryable
evidence: DataLoaderTest.java verifies each dataset loads successfully; all 6 datasets tested individually; 23954 total matches and 18207 players loaded successfully

title: At least 20 sample questions can be answered
evidence: 7 MCP tools exposed: search_matches, head_to_head, get_team_stats, get_standings, search_players, get_competition_stats, get_biggest_wins; MatchToolsTest and TeamToolsTest cover 20+ test scenarios

title: Cross-file queries work (player + match data)
evidence: DataRepository.java unifies access to all matches and players; tools can correlate match and player data; tests exercise combined data access

title: Full MCP Protocol implementation
evidence: BrazilianSoccerMcpServer.java uses official MCP SDK with McpServer.sync() and StdioServerTransportProvider; proper tool registration with schema and parameters
suggestion: Implementation exceeds spec by properly implementing MCP protocol

title: Comprehensive test suite with BDD patterns
evidence: 5 test classes covering all major components; 44 tests, 0 failures, 0 skipped; BDD-style test names and comments in MatchToolsTest, DataLoaderTest, TeamToolsTest, PlayerToolsTest, TeamNameNormalizerTest
suggestion: Test coverage exceeds typical expectations for this specification

title: Date format handling for historical data
evidence: DataLoader.java implements date conversion from DD/MM/YYYY to YYYY-MM-DD format; DataLoaderTest::testLoadHistoricalMatches verifies conversion accuracy; supports multiple date formats
suggestion: Implementation handles multiple date formats beyond spec requirement

title: UTF-8 character encoding support
evidence: DataLoader.java uses StandardCharsets.UTF_8 for file reading (line 14); TeamNameNormalizer.java handles accented characters (São Paulo, Grêmio, Avaí)
suggestion: Proper UTF-8 handling ensures Portuguese character support
